misconceptions: DBQ: using documents as illustrations only, without sourcing/HIPP
LEQ: thesis that restates the prompt without a line of reasoning
Treating “Europe” as static geography across periods
Confusing correlation in stimulus data with causation
paper_rules: MCQ: stimulus sets; select evidence supporting historical claims
SAQ: concise A/B/C responses with specific evidence
DBQ: thesis, contextualization, document use, sourcing, complexity as scored
LEQ: choose prompt; thesis + specific evidence + historical reasoning
units_summary: U1 Renaissance and Exploration c.1450–1648 (w≈0.1, q=50)
U2 Age of Reformation c.1450–1648 (w≈0.1, q=50)
U3 Absolutism and Constitutionalism c.1648–1815 (w≈0.11, q=55)
U4 Scientific Philosophical and Political Developments c.1648–1815 (w≈0.11, q=55)
U5 Conflict Crisis and Reaction in the Late 18th Century (w≈0.12, q=60)
U6 Industrialization and Its Effects c.1815–1914 (w≈0.12, q=60)
U7 19th-Century Perspectives and Political Developments (w≈0.12, q=60)
U9 Cold War and Contemporary Europe (w≈0.1, q=50)
